questions.question:
Given that
B(a) means "a is a bear"
F(a) means "a is a fish" and
E(a,b) means "a eats b"
Then what is the best meaning of
\(\forall x[F(x) \rightarrow \forall y(E(y, x) \rightarrow b(y))]\)

(ISRO CSE 2020)
options:
A.

Every fish is eaten by some bear
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
For the distributions given below :

Which of the following is correct for the above distributions?

(ISRO CSE 2020)
options:
A.

Standard deviation of A is significantly lower than standard deviation of B

B.

Standard deviation of A is slightly lower than standard deviation of B

C.

Standard deviation of A is same as standard deviation of B

D.

Standard deviation of A is significantly higher than standard deviation of B
questions.correct_answer:
C.

Standard deviation of A is same as standard deviation of B

questions.question:
(G,*) is an abelian group. Then

(ISRO CSE 2018)
options:
A.

\(x=x^{-1}\) for any x belonging to G

B.

\(x=x^{2}\) for any x belonging to G

C.

\((x * y)^{2}=x^{2} * y^{2}\) , for any x,y belonging to G
questions.correct_answer:
C.

\((x * y)^{2}=x^{2} * y^{2}\) , for any x,y belonging to G
